reasoning: The threshold value on 2025-08-31 is 11.67%. This figure reflects a sudden weekend month-end jump from 11.46% on the preceding Friday. For the earliest resolution date (2025-09-07), the underlying daily yield is starting approx. 20 bps lower, and the prevailing short-term trend has been downward leading up to the September FOMC meeting; thus, the probability of exceeding 11.67% within just a week is modeled as quite low (31%), closely aligning with the FRED linear trend forecast. For the medium-to-long horizons (2025-09-30, 2025-11-29, and 2026-02-27), uncertainty significantly increases. High-yield CCC spreads are highly volatile. While the Fed is heavily predicted (87% odds via CME FedWatch) to cut rates due to weak jobs data in September, worsening macroeconomic indicators could prompt recessionary fears and trigger significant risk-off widening of CCC credit spreads, well outweighing any baseline Treasury yield drops. Additionally, 11-12% is near the lower boundary of historical norms for this specific high yield index. Consequently, the probability steadily rises toward and slightly above 50% for the most distant resolution date due to high variance and mean-reversion expectations.
